originalTitle: Prediction market firms face growing scrutiny as Congress weighs regulation
summary: Rep. Bryan Steil, R-Wisc., is working on a bill to ban Congress members and their staff from certain bets on prediction markets such as Kalshi and Polymarket.
content:
The leader of the congressional committee that oversees how the House functions says he is working on legislation to not only ban current lawmakers from making prediction markets bets on elections and politics but that he wants to expand that to former federal lawmakers and candidates for those offices.

Rep. Bryan Steil , R-Wisc., who chairs the House administration committee, told reporters this week he doesn't see an issue in lawmakers betting on nonpolitical events, like the outcome of sports games. But betting on elections and policies — topics where they could have access to insider information — would be banned on platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket .

"There's an avenue to make sure, now that these new and kind of novel markets are available to consumers writ large, that we put that in the rules explicitly," he told reporters.

Kalshi and Polymarket officials, as well as some traders on the site, met with lawmakers this week as part of a larger effort to make their case about how Washington interacts with prediction markets. In addition to legislation to address insider trading risks, lawmakers are considering how to deal with prediction markets based outside the U.S.

While the Senate already changed its rules to ban senators and their staffers from placing bets on prediction markets, the House is looking to write a more tailored ban into law.

Although insider trading laws already exist, Steil said it's become critical for lawmakers to avoid the appearance of using their elected office to personally profit.

Steil, who is working with House GOP leadership on his bill, plans to attach the language to legislation banning lawmakers from buying stocks. That bill advanced out of committee earlier this year but has yet to get a vote on the House floor despite promises from leadership that it would.

Steil said he also wants to see prediction markets that "are regulated in the United States, are under the values of the United States, and that we protect consumers from external transactions."

Such a change could affect the largest prediction market, Polymarket, which currently bans U.S. users from its main exchange, although the company is working to get a legal foothold in the U.S.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission regulates prediction markets and has argued in courts and public statements that it, rather than other federal agencies or states, should have exclusive regulatory authority over the industry.

Polymarket and Kalshi supported the Senate's move to bar lawmakers from making bets. Kalshi has also flagged and fined political candidates who have used insider knowledge of their own races to make bets.

While Steil is planning to introduce his member-betting legislation soon, broader bills regulating the market could take longer to achieve. Those would be under the purview of either the House Agriculture Committee or the House Financial Services Committee.

While time is quickly running out for legislation this year, there's interest among Democrats to put their own restrictions in place. When asked about the Senate's rule change during a CNBC CEO Council Summit , Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, D-N.Y., said he thought the House "will do the same thing."

Disclosure: CNBC and Kalshi have a commercial relationship that includes customer acquisition and a minority investment.
chineseTitle: 随着国会权衡监管，预测市场公司面临越来越严格的审查
chineseSummary: 威斯康星州共和党众议员 Bryan Steil 正在制定一项法案，禁止国会议员及其工作人员对 Kalshi 和 Polymarket 等预测市场进行某些押注。
chineseContent:
负责监督众议院运作的国会委员会领导人表示，他正在制定立法，不仅禁止现任议员对选举和政治进行预测市场押注，而且他希望将这一范围扩大到前联邦议员和这些职位的候选人。

众议院管理委员会主席、威斯康星州共和党众议员布莱恩·斯泰尔本周对记者表示，他认为立法者押注非政治事件（例如体育比赛的结果）没有问题。但在 Kalshi 和 Polymarket 等平台上，将禁止对选举和政策进行投注——他们可以获取内幕信息的话题。

他告诉记者：“现在有一种途径可以确保消费者可以大量接触到这些新奇的市场，我们可以将其明确纳入规则中。”

Kalshi 和 Polymarket 的官员以及该网站上的一些交易员本周会见了立法者，作为就华盛顿如何与预测市场互动的更大努力的一部分。除了立法解决内幕交易风险外，立法者还在考虑如何应对美国境外的预测市场。

虽然参议院已经修改了规则，禁止参议员及其工作人员在预测市场上下注，但众议院正在寻求将一项更有针对性的禁令写入法律。

尽管内幕交易法已经存在，但斯泰尔表示，立法者必须避免出现利用当选职位谋取个人利益的现象。

斯泰尔正在与众议院共和党领导层就其法案进行合作，他计划将这一措辞附加到禁止立法者购买股票的立法中。该法案于今年早些时候通过了委员会，但尚未在众议院获得投票，尽管领导层承诺会这样做。

斯泰尔表示，他还希望看到预测市场“在美国受到监管，遵循美国的价值观，并且我们保护消费者免受外部交易的影响”。

这样的变化可能会影响最大的预测市场 Polymarket，该公司目前禁止美国用户使用其主要交易所，尽管该公司正在努力在美国获得合法立足点。

商品期货交易委员会负责监管预测市场，并在法庭和公开声明中辩称，它而不是其他联邦机构或州应该对该行业拥有专属监管权。

Polymarket 和 Kalshi 支持参议院禁止议员下注的举措。卡尔希还对那些利用自己种族的内幕知识进行投注的政治候选人进行了标记和罚款。

虽然斯泰尔计划很快推出会员博彩立法，但更广泛的市场监管法案可能需要更长的时间才能实现。这些将属于众议院农业委员会或众议院金融服务委员会的职权范围。

虽然今年立法的时间很快就过去了，但民主党人有兴趣实施自己的限制。当在 CNBC 首席执行官委员会峰会期间被问及参议院规则变化时，纽约州民主党少数党领袖哈基姆·杰弗里斯 (Hakeem Jeffries) 表示，他认为众议院“也会做同样的事情”。

披露：CNBC 和 Kalshi 之间存在商业关系，包括客户获取和少数股权投资。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
